next_steps: Skin biopsy of purpuric lesions for histopathology and tissue culture
Bronchoscopy with bronchoalveolar lavage (BAL) for bacterial, fungal, viral, and parasitic studies (specifically looking for Strongyloides larvae)
Stool examination for ova and parasites (specifically looking for Strongyloides larvae)
Peripheral blood smear to evaluate for microangiopathic hemolytic anemia
Autoimmune serology (ANCA, C3/C4) to investigate potential vasculitis
Complete coagulation panel and serum haptoglobin/LDH to evaluate for DIC or TMA
